differences.detail: Production is a narrow, dense mobile stack focused on header + player cards, while prototype uses a wider canvas with many structured sections (overview, draft, graph, events, tables, timeline). Overall composition and section flow do not match.
Production uses tighter vertical rhythm and smaller inter-section gaps; prototype uses larger outer margins, card padding, and clearer whitespace separation between modules.
Font sizing/weight hierarchy differs: production has heavier, flatter text treatment; prototype uses more differentiated heading/body scales and clearer section-title emphasis.

Accent palette is different (production leans green/yellow/coral; prototype leans brand red + neutral grays + controlled green/red state colors). Contrast tuning and highlight semantics are inconsistent.
Prototype applies larger, more consistent card and chip corner radii; production has smaller/mixed radii across tabs, rows, and cards.
Prototype uses stronger depth cues (soft glow/overlay layering and textured background), while production appears flatter with minimal elevation separation.
Prototype features larger team logos and consistently sized icon systems across modules; production uses smaller logos/icons and lacks comparable visual prominence.
Prototype has a clear top-down hierarchy and progressive disclosure across sections; production jumps quickly into detailed rows, reducing scanability and section-level orientation.

top_3_fixes: Match the prototype layout structure first: same screen composition, section order, and spacing scale (outer margins, card paddings, inter-section gaps).
Unify design tokens to prototype: typography scale/weights, brand-accent mapping, neutral text colors, and consistent state colors.
Align component visuals: card/chip radius values, elevation/shadow treatment, and logo/icon sizing rules for stronger hierarchy.
